resource.name: A reference to a document
resource.description: This resource identifies the healthcare documentation created for a Patient in ARIA

resource.name: activityAdditionalPerformer
resource.description: Performer of the Activity of Care Plan.

resource.name: activityBasedOn
resource.description: The predecessor activity this care plan activity is based on.

resource.name: ActivityDefinition
resource.description: This resource allows for the definition of some activity to be performed, it may be used to specify actions to be taken as part of a workflow, appointment or tasks.

resource.description: ActivityDefinition example for the *Varian ARIA API ActivityDefinition Profile.*

resource.name: activityDefinition-category
resource.description: Classification of Activity.

resource.name: address-telephone1

resource.name: AllergyIntolerance
resource.description:
AllergyIntolerance represents Patient's Allergies in ARIA.

All recorded allergies or NO KNOWN ALLERGY status will be reflected using this.

resource.name: AllergyIntolerance ARIA Category
resource.description:
This value set defines different allergy types configured in Aria. 
List of configured allergy categories are available under valueset 'aria-allergy-category'. 
All values can be accessed using $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/allergy-intolerance-category.

resource.name: allergyIntolerance-allergyCategoryInAria
resource.description: ARIA Coded Allergy category.

resource.name: allergyIntolerance-reaction-lifeThreatening
resource.description: ARIA allergy reaction.

resource.name: Appointment
resource.description: A booking of a healthcare event among patient(s), practitioner(s), related person(s) and/or device(s) for a specific date/time. This may result in one or more Encounter(s).

resource.name: Appointment Example
resource.description: Appointment example for the *Varian ARIA API Appointment Profile.*

resource.name: appointment-actualTime
resource.description: Actual Start date & Actual End date for Appointment.

resource.name: appointment-ariaStatus
resource.description: ARIA Specific Status.

resource.name: appointment-visitInstructions
resource.description: Visit instructions of a MO appointment

resource.name: appointment-visitInstructions
resource.description: Visit instructions of a MO appointment

resource.name: approvalDate
resource.description: The date on which the Physical Exam record approved.

resource.name: assertedOn
resource.description: Date on which observation result value has been approved.

resource.name: Audit Event
resource.description: A record of an event made for purposes of maintaining a security log. Typical uses include detection of intrusion attempts and monitoring for inappropriate usage.

resource.name: AuditEvent Example
resource.description: AuditEvent example for the *Varian ARIA API AuditEvent Profile.*

resource.name: BodyStructure
resource.description: This resource record details about an anatomical structure. This resource may be used when a coded concept does not provide the necessary detail needed for the use case.

resource.name: CarePlan
resource.description:
Care Plan profile is derived from FHIR Domain Resource.
It represents care plan in ARIA .

resource.name: CarePlan Template
resource.description:
The configured careplan template in Aria. 
List of configured templates are accessible using valueset 'aria-careplan-template'. All values can be accessed using  $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/careplan-template

resource.name: CareTeam
resource.description:
This Resource includes all the people and organizations who plan to participate in the coordination and delivery of care for a patient.

Careteam represents department and practitioner (doctor/staff) assigned to the Patient in ARIA.

ARIA does not configure predefined teams, so single Careteam per Patient will represent associations with Department and practitioner.

resource.name: CareTeam Example
resource.description: CareTeam example for the *Varian ARIA API CareTeam Profile.*

resource.name: ChargeItem
resource.description: This Resource represents charges captured for an activity.

resource.name: condition-assertedDate

resource.name: Condition-Diagnosis (CancerCondition)
resource.description: This resource is used to record detailed information about a  diagnosis that has risen to a level of concern. The condition could be a point in time diagnosis in context of an encounter, it could be an item on the practitioner's Problem List, or it could be a concern that doesn't exist on the practitioner's Problem List.

resource.name: condition-isExternal
resource.description: Indicates that the diagnosis was brought in via interface (i.e. it was done external to our application).

resource.name: condition-laterality
resource.description: Designated codes for condition side of the body affected.

resource.name: condition-rank
resource.description: Concepts that identify the significance or priority of the diagnosis code. Note that the codes are numeric, and the number of the code represents the ordinal priority of the associated diagnosis.

resource.name: Condition-Stage-isWorkingStage
resource.description: Signifies if the observation is in woking group.

resource.name: Country
resource.description:
This value set defines a base set of codes for countries configured in Aria. 
The list of country accepted under citizenShip are available under ValueSet 'aria-country'. 
All values can be accessed using  $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/country

resource.name: customAttributes
resource.description: Configurable Attributes on Patient. ARIA has Configurable User defined Attributes to accommodate Patient's properties which are not present in ARIA out of box.

resource.name: daily-dose-limit
resource.description: Daily dose limit

resource.name: default
resource.description: The resource is default choice among similar set

resource.name: defaultServiceOrganization

resource.name: Device
resource.description: This resource identifies an instance or a type of a manufactured item that is used in the provision of healthcare without being substantially changed through that activity. The device may be a medical or non-medical device.  Medical devices include durable (reusable) medical equipment, implantable devices, as well as disposable equipment used for diagnostic, treatment, and research for healthcare and public health.  Non-medical devices may include items such as a machine, cellphone, computer, application, etc.

resource.name: Device Example
resource.description: Device example for the *Varian ARIA API Device Profile.*

resource.name: Device Types
resource.description: List of configured device types are accessible using valueset 'aria-device-type'. All values can be accessed using  $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/device-type

resource.name: Diagnosis Code
resource.description: List of available codes are accessible using valueset 'aria-diagnosiscode'. All values can be accessed using ValueSet $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/condition-code. Codes can be filtered on schemas using context filter i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/condition-code&context=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-9-cm|icd-9-cm

resource.name: Group
resource.description:
Group represents logical grouping of Resources like Advance Practitioners, Nurses,Oncologists, System administrators etc...      
Group comprises Doctor/Staff (Practitioner), Machine (Device) and Venue (Location) with in a department in ARIA.

resource.name: Group Example
resource.description: Group example for the *Varian ARIA API Group Profile.*

resource.name: HealthcareService
resource.description:
HealthcareService represents departments in ARIA as in ARIA department and services are not two separate concepts.
Although Department are already being represented by Organization resource, since many other FHIR clinical resources works on HealthcareService concept, we are mapping department in Healthcare service as well.

resource.name: HealthcareService Example
resource.description: HealthcareService example for the *Varian ARIA API HealthcareService Profile.*

resource.name: isChargeCaptureApplicable
resource.description: Flag in ARIA to define charge capture after completion of a service in Facility.

resource.name: Location
resource.description:
Location includes both incidental locations (a place which is used for healthcare ) and dedicated, formally appointed locations.
It can be of Auxiliary type or Venue type. 
Auxiliary locations include imaging machines and treatment machines.
Venues are locations within a department like treatment room, waiting room, consultation room, etc.  Some locations can be schedulable.

resource.name: Location Example
resource.description: Location example for the *Varian ARIA API Location Profile.*

resource.description: Associated department in ARIA (Supported only on ARIA V18 or onwards)

resource.name: Observation Stage Category Example
resource.description: Observation Stage Category example with all attributes specified.

resource.name: Observation-StageCategory
resource.description:
Observations are a central element in healthcare, used to support diagnosis, monitor progress, determine baselines and patterns and even capture demographic   characteristics. Most observations are simple name/value pair assertions with some metadata, but some observations group other observations together logically, or even are multi-component observations. 

This profile is used to represent the Stage Category in Condition Diagnosis resource.

resource.name: observation-stageClassification
resource.description: Stage Classification, clinical, pathological, posttherapy, recurrence, autopsy is called the Classification.

resource.name: Observation-StageGroup
resource.description:
Observations are a central element in healthcare, used to support diagnosis, monitor progress, determine baselines and patterns and even capture demographic   characteristics. Most observations are simple name/value pair assertions with some metadata, but some observations group other observations together logically, or even are multi-component observations. 

This profile is used to represent the Stage Group in Condition Diagnosis resource.

resource.name: Organization
resource.description:
A formally or informally recognized grouping of organizations formed for the purpose of achieving some form of collective action. Includes institutions, departments etc

Organization represents Hospital and Departments configured in ARIA. A Hospital can have multiple departments.

Hospital is represented with category 'prov (Provider)' and Department is represented with category 'dept (Department)'. 

Hospital and Department relationship is represented on Department.

resource.description: Organization Hospital example for the *Varian ARIA API Organization Hospital Profile.*

resource.name: Practitioner
resource.description:
Practitioner covers all individuals who are engaged in the healthcare process and healthcare-related services as part of their formal responsibilities and this Resource is used for attribution of activities and responsibilities to these individuals. Practitioners include (but are not limited to):

physicians, dentists, pharmacists, 
physician assistants, nurses, scribes, 
midwives, dietitians, therapists, optometrists, paramedics, 
medical technicians, laboratory scientists, prosthetic technicians, radiographers, 
social workers, professional homecare providers, official volunteers, 
receptionists handling patient registration

Practitioner represents Doctor and Staff configured in ARIA. 

Doctor/Staff demographics, role and  association with department is being represented.

resource.name: Practitioner Example
resource.description: Practitioner example for the *Varian ARIA API Practitioner Profile.*

resource.name: Practitioner Ids
resource.description: List of practitioner ids configured are available under valueset 'aria-practitioner-ids'. All values can be accessed using  $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/practitioner-ids

resource.name: Practitioner Role
resource.description: The different practitioner roles configured are available under valueset 'aria-practitioner-role'. All values can be accessed using  $expand operation i.e. /fhir/ValueSet/$expand?url=http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/practitioner-role

resource.name: Radiotherapy Bolus
resource.description: Bolus used (or to be used) for radiotherapy.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Course Prescription
resource.description:
The elements of a treatment course that can be defined during prescription. This represents the treatment course in ARIA, not an actual prescription and is the parent for all presciptions in the course.
It contains the data elements of a course that are not specific to planning or delivery, for example, treatment intent, relation to the diagnosis, or course status.
Those elements are exposed as CodeX RT Radiotherapy Course Prescription although ARIA does not have an actual prescription for the complete course. See the Phase Prescription profile for actual prescriptions in ARIA.
With the Radiotherapy Course Prescription, elements of the course can be found as soon as a course is defined in ARIA, even if no treatment plans or delivered treatments are available yet.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Course Summary
resource.description:
A summary of the treatment progress in a radiotherapy course of treatment.
When additional treatments are delivered in the same course, this resource is updated. No new resource is created.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Patient Example
resource.description: Example radiotherapy patient.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Phase Prescription
resource.description:
A prescription for a radiotherapy treatment phase.
In ARIA, a prescription can cover a single plan or complete phase implemented with one or multiple plans. During prescription it may not even be defined whether
multiple plans will be used. Therefore, all ARIA prescriptions are exposed as Phase Prescriptions.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Planned Course
resource.description: A summary of all plans in a radiotherapy course of treatment. Only approved plans with status other than Unapproved, Rejected, Reviewed are considered.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Planned Phase
resource.description:
A summary of all plans in a radiotherapy treatment phase.
In ARIA, all plans linked to the same prescription are considered as part of the same phase.
One Radiotherapy Planned Phase is exposed for each ARIA prescription that has at least one linked plan in an approved status (other than Unapproved, Rejected, Reviewed).

resource.name: Radiotherapy Prescription Energy Example
resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Energy

resource.name: Radiotherapy Prescription Energy Example
resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Energy

resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Modality

resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Site

resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Site

resource.name: Radiotherapy Prescription Technique Example
resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Technique.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Prescription Technique Example
resource.description: Radiotherapy Prescription Technique.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Treated Phase
resource.description: A summary of the treatment progress in a radiotherapy treatment phase. In ARIA, all plans linked to the same prescription are considered as part of the same phase.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Treated Plan
resource.description: A summary of the treatment progress in one radiotherapy treatment plan.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Treatment Plan
resource.description: A radiotherapy treatment plan.

resource.name: Radiotherapy Volume
resource.description:
A volume of the body used in radiotherapy planning or treatment delivery.
In ARIA, dose is specified and tracked against Reference Points. These Reference Points can represent actual points or three-dimensional volumes.
ARIA calls all of those 'Reference Point', even if not a point. Each Reference Point in ARIA is exposed as a Radiotherapy Volume.

resource.description: Example BodyStructure compliant to the RadiotherapyVolume profile.

resource.name: Task
resource.description:
Task resource represents tasks configured in ARIA.   


A task resource describes an activity that can be performed and tracks the state of completion of that activity. It is a representation that an activity should be or has been initiated, and eventually, represents the successful or unsuccessful completion of that activity.

resource.name: ValueSet
resource.description:
* ValueSet specifies a set of codes drawn from one or more code systems, intended for use in a particular context. Value sets link between CodeSystem definitions and their use in coded elements.

* ^publisher = "Varian ARIA API Profile Team"

* Value sets have 2 aspects:

    * .compose:  A definition of which codes are intended to be in the value set ("intension") -/li-
    *  .expansion: The list of codes that are actually in the value set under a given set of conditions ("extension") -/li-


* Please refer the How to -> Valueset & Codesystem List Page for list of valueset and codesystems.
